<div className="sb-intro-page">

# Welcome to the YaleSites Design System

<p className="lead-text">
  This is the comprehensive component library and design system for Yale
  University websites, built on atomic design principles.
</p>

## About This Design System

The YaleSites Design System provides a consistent, accessible, and flexible foundation for building websites across Yale University. It includes reusable components, design tokens, and comprehensive documentation to ensure brand consistency and excellent user experiences.

The design system was developed in close collaboration with the **University Office of Public Affairs and Communication (OPAC)** and built in tandem with the [YaleSites Drupal platform](https://yalesites.yale.edu). While the system was created with Drupal development in mind, it is intentionally technology-agnostic — it can serve as a design reference or be adopted in other technology stacks.

---

## Atomic Design Principles

Our component library is organized using **Atomic Design** methodology, breaking interfaces down into fundamental building blocks:

<div style={{display: 'grid', gridTemplateColumns: 'repeat(2, 1fr)', gap: '2rem', margin: '2rem 0'}}>

<div
  style={{
    textAlign: 'center',
    padding: '1.5rem',
    background: '#f5f5f5',
    borderRadius: '8px',
  }}
>
  <div style={{ fontSize: '2rem', marginBottom: '0.5rem' }}>⚛️</div>
  <h3 style={{ margin: '0.5rem 0' }}>Atoms</h3>
  <p style={{ fontSize: '0.9rem', margin: '0' }}>
    The foundational building blocks - buttons, links, inputs, headings, images,
    and other basic HTML elements that can't be broken down further.
  </p>
</div>

<div
  style={{
    textAlign: 'center',
    padding: '1.5rem',
    background: '#f5f5f5',
    borderRadius: '8px',
  }}
>
  <div style={{ fontSize: '2rem', marginBottom: '0.5rem' }}>🧪</div>
  <h3 style={{ margin: '0.5rem 0' }}>Molecules</h3>
  <p style={{ fontSize: '0.9rem', margin: '0' }}>
    Simple groups of atoms functioning together - cards, forms, search bars,
    navigation items, and other relatively simple components.
  </p>
</div>

<div
  style={{
    textAlign: 'center',
    padding: '1.5rem',
    background: '#f5f5f5',
    borderRadius: '8px',
  }}
>
  <div style={{ fontSize: '2rem', marginBottom: '0.5rem' }}>⚙️</div>
  <h3 style={{ margin: '0.5rem 0' }}>Organisms</h3>
  <p style={{ fontSize: '0.9rem', margin: '0' }}>
    Complex components made of molecules and atoms - headers, footers, card
    collections, galleries, and other substantial interface sections.
  </p>
</div>

<div
  style={{
    textAlign: 'center',
    padding: '1.5rem',
    background: '#f5f5f5',
    borderRadius: '8px',
  }}
>
  <div style={{ fontSize: '2rem', marginBottom: '0.5rem' }}>📄</div>
  <h3 style={{ margin: '0.5rem 0' }}>Pages</h3>
  <p style={{ fontSize: '0.9rem', margin: '0' }}>
    Complete page layouts and examples combining organisms, molecules, and atoms
    into fully functional page templates and demonstrations.
  </p>
</div>

</div>

---

## Organization Structure

- **Tokens**: Design system tokens including colors, typography, spacing, and effects
- **Atoms**: Basic elements like buttons, links, images, and form controls
- **Molecules**: Component combinations like cards, banners, navigation items, and content blocks
- **Organisms**: Complex sections like headers, footers, menus, and content collections
- **Page Layouts**: Page structure templates with various column configurations
- **Page Examples**: Complete page demonstrations showing real-world implementations

---

## Component Documentation Structure

The YaleSites Component Library uses a modern two-page documentation structure:

### Documentation Page (MDX)

Educational content with component introduction, interactive preview with controls, property documentation, theme options, key variants, technical specifications, and usage guidelines.

### Visual Regression Page (Visreg)

Comprehensive testing page with interactive controls and all component variations for Percy visual regression testing coverage.

For more details, see the [Storybook Migration Guide](https://github.com/yalesites-org/component-library-twig/blob/main/STORYBOOK-MIGRATION-GUIDE.md).
